title: Oat and castile body wash
subtitle: A gentle, diluted castile soap body wash with colloidal oatmeal and lavender
excerpt: Diluted castile soap with colloidal oatmeal and vegetable glycerine makes a gentle, low-stripping body wash suited to normal and sensitive skin. The oatmeal suspends evenly in the diluted soap and adds a silky feel in the lather. A broad-spectrum preservative is required because the wash contains water. Yield is approximately 200 ml.
sourceNotes: Castile soap dilution for body wash: Dr. Bronner's guide recommends diluting liquid castile at 1:3 (soap to water) for body wash. This recipe uses a higher soap ratio (135g soap to 45g water) for a richer lather. Colloidal oatmeal in wash-off products: FDA-recognised skin protectant; at 4% in a wash-off product, provides soothing benefit. Vegetable glycerine as humectant: draws moisture to the skin; 4% in a wash-off product is within typical use ranges. Broad-spectrum preservative: any water-containing personal care product requires a preservative system; add at 1% of total batch weight.

recipe.yieldDescription: Approximately 200 ml (fills 2 x 100 ml pump-dispenser bottles)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. One pump-dispenser bottle per 100 ml of finished wash.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Label with the batch date and use-by date.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months with the broad-spectrum preservative. Discard if the wash develops mould, changes colour, or develops an off smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: An ingredient that attracts and holds moisture. Vegetable glycerine is a humectant: it draws water from the air and from deeper skin layers to the surface, helping the skin feel soft after washing even in a rinse-off product.

content.text: Diluted castile soap makes a gentle body wash. Diluting the concentrated soap softens the cleansing action and gives a lather that cleans without stripping the skin. Colloidal oatmeal at 4% makes the lather feel slightly silky. Vegetable glycerine acts as a
, leaving a faint moisture film after rinsing. This wash contains water, so a broad-spectrum preservative is required: without one, bacteria and mould can start to grow in days.

items.prepNote: Unscented liquid castile soap; diluted in this recipe
Use distilled or boiled and cooled water
Finely milled cosmetic grade; contains gluten
Humectant; helps skin retain moisture after washing
1% of total batch weight; follow manufacturer instructions for type and dose

content.text: In a clean bowl, combine the castile soap and distilled water. Stir gently to mix; do not shake or whisk as this creates foam. Add the colloidal oatmeal and vegetable glycerine and stir until the oatmeal is dispersed evenly. Add the broad-spectrum preservative and stir again. Finally, add the lavender essential oil and stir to combine.
Use the funnel to decant the wash into the pump-dispenser bottles. Label with the batch date and use-by date (3 months from today). The wash will be fluid; it does not thicken significantly.

Store at room temperature away from direct sunlight. With the broad-spectrum preservative, the wash keeps 3 months. Discard if it develops mould, changes colour, or smells off. Shake gently before use to redistribute the oatmeal.
